item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Two things: the town "Chilliwhack' at the end is actually spelled without the last H, also kanterfaas cheese should be kanterkaas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if the phrase "very, very worrisome" is very appropriate for the tone of SCPs. It doesn't sound professional at all.

item.title: the skip
item.content:encoded:
Frick, this freaked me out so much
+1

one complaint: at the end of the interview log, the classification of it as a memetic and biohazard felt a little forced.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Concept 1 is up for feedback-ing.

http://scpsandbox2.wikidot.com/acal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it somewhat; thought it was a good mix between humorous, disgusting, and a bit horrifying even. Both the end of the interview (which seemed too absurd to be honest) and the incident log brought the article down a bit to where I'd have to no-vote. I like the style you went for here though, and I hope you continue to write more. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the feedback! Anything you can tell me that can help me improve is greatly appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: just… just no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ACal I'll be brie f, the scip was pretty gouda, I curd give it a +1 but the premise is still a bit soft. It doesn't stink, but I can't bring myself to salut it. Perhaps, with a bit more age it will sharpen up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cheers!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A single small wheel, weighing approximately 0.4 kilograms, forms at the top of the pile, with a single label marked Terrence Farms Gorgonzola - Perfect for spreading!

You clever bastard. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, but which statement? Is it the expunged part in the description?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this is just shy of funny enough to updoot. What really shines here is the first half of the testing log, and the interview; but that last [REDACTD]-laden statement killed it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a great balance between serious and goofy. It is amusing to read but it also has a serious tone to it. It held my interest throughout the entire article. +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I like this. Upvoted. Reminds me of SCP-420 *(not j).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neither of those hooks are particularly new. See SCP-2680 for a recent example of the first and… I mean, just about any fifthist or factory skip for the second. There's no real depth to Terrance farms, except that the cheeses keep showing up, basically just making it a ticking clock component.

Not that there being depth to Terrance farms would have saved this for me.

It's not even the only cheese skip, we've got the pun -J at the least.

This is an old, tired story. It's very well written, but not exceptionally so. It would have to be something spectacular, or at least with a substantial twist, for me to upvote something like this.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is too bizarre not to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Brackish; water, slightly salty or briny/distasteful or unpleasant. So no, that isn't misused.

It's not a cheese that makes you eat cheese until you die; that's a side effect of the initial infection. The hook consists of two things, the first being that the cheese recreates itself through infected subjects, and second being that an unknown individual or group of individuals is spreading it with malicious intent. I'm sorry that I haven't made this clear enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll keep 'em coming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tread Caerphilly

Fuck it, I'm now upvoting on the basis of these puns, god damn it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, thank you! Also, the draft for the first tale surrounding Rho-87, titled "Tread Caerphilly" will be up in a couple of weeks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy shit, you wrote it.

Holy shit, this is good, too!

Hot damn, nice job, ACal :U

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Forgot to mention this, but thanks to DrMagnus for the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm quite proud of that little pun. Also, I'm not sure that I'm getting this across in the article, but I'm trying to make the main focal point the group that's manufacturing the cheese. I might try to touch that up a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, go right ahead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was posted at a really unfortunate time (as it's getting drowned out by really, really high quality 3000 entries) but I actually really enjoyed it - a well-needed break of lightheartedness in the sea of horror.

While the effect itself isn't that interesting - cheese that makes you want cheese - I find the article whimsical enough and quite enjoyable.

And "No, Not the Cheese" is a title worthy of an upvote on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cheesy. I see what you did there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually really, really like this. It is goofy and cheesy, but I still like it. However, the stages and interview can be kinda hard to read without any spaces. Mind if I fix the format a bit?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, I see them. Cheers for letting me know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2867 is a compulsive memetic and physical infection surrounding an unknown amount of gorgonzola cheese

what

I'm okay with this, but you've got a couple SCP-X's in there still.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you're gonna do a scip with a compulsion effect, it's gonna be a tough sell unless you do something nobody's seen before, because compulsion effects are cliché nowadays. Unfortunately, this doesn't strike me as very interesting or memorable. It's cheese that makes you want cheese, and then makes you make cheese. And then you eat cheese till you die. Sorry, -1.

P.S. You also committed my pet peeve: you've misused the word "brackish." Don't know why that bugs me but it does. ;)
